voting_steps.title: Voting Day Procedure
data: Check your name in the voter list
Carry voter ID or approved ID proof

eligibility_rules.title: Voter Eligibility Rules
data: Must be registered in the electoral roll

voter_registration: Visit the NVSP portal or voter service center
Fill Form 6 for new voter registration
evm_explanation: Electronic Voting Machines (EVM) are used for voting in Indian elections
Voters press a button next to their chosen candidate
The machine records the vote electronically
A VVPAT slip briefly displays the chosen candidate
The slip is stored securely for audit verification

voting_preparation.title: How to Prepare for Voting
data: Check your name in the electoral roll
Carry voter ID or an approved identity document
Reach the polling station during voting hours

polling_day_process.title: Polling Day Process
data: Enter polling station and join the queue
Your finger is marked with indelible ink
You proceed to the voting compartment
Press the button for your chosen candidate on the EVM
VVPAT briefly displays your vote confirmation

faq.who_conducts_elections: The Election Commission of India conducts national and state elections.
faq.can_i_vote_without_voter_id: Yes, alternative approved identity documents may be accepted at polling stations.
faq.can_i_vote_twice: No. Each registered voter can vote only once in an election.
faq.what_is_nota: NOTA stands for 'None of the Above'. It is an option on the voting machine that allows voters to reject all candidates in their constituency.
faq.role_of_election_commission: The Election Commission of India is an autonomous constitutional authority responsible for administering election processes, ensuring free and fair elections.
faq.what_is_vvpat: VVPAT (Voter Verifiable Paper Audit Trail) is a system attached to the EVM that prints a paper slip displaying the voter's choice, allowing them to verify their vote.
faq.how_are_votes_counted: Votes are counted electronically from the EVMs under high security, in the presence of returning officers and candidate representatives.

election_timeline_detailed: Election Commission announces the election schedule
Model Code of Conduct comes into effect
polling_booth_guidance: Check your polling booth location on the Election Commission website or NVSP portal
Enter your voter ID or personal details
Note the address and polling time
Carry your voter ID or an approved identity document when visiting the booth
polling_booth_finder: Visit the NVSP portal or Election Commission website
Enter your voter ID or personal details
Note the polling booth address and voting time
Carry valid ID when visiting the booth
candidate_info: Candidate information is available on the Election Commission website
election_facts: India has the largest democratic electorate in the world, with over 970 million registered voters.
The Indian general election is conducted in multiple phases spread across several weeks.
EVMs (Electronic Voting Machines) have been used in Indian elections since 1982.
The Model Code of Conduct prohibits political parties from making new policy announcements during election campaigns.
The Election Commission of India was established on January 25, 1950 — one day before the Republic.
India uses a first-past-the-post (FPTP) voting system for Lok Sabha elections.
NOTA (None of the Above) was introduced in Indian elections in 2013.
Indelible ink used in Indian elections was first used in the 1962 general election.
Indian voters as young as 18 years old have the right to vote since the 61st Constitutional Amendment of 1989.
The Election Commission can postpone or cancel polls in a constituency if law and order cannot be maintained.
